title: Two verbs: compact-log and compact-check
action: Per D3/D17/D24. Register TWO verbs only — `state compact-log --event <precompact|resume> --session-id <id> [--json]` and `state compact-check --session-id <id> [--json]` — following state.handoff.write (command-registry.mjs:877-905 entry shape; handler in skills/bee-hive/templates/bee.mjs; one line in the name-to-handler map at bee.mjs:4992-4994). `state compact-capsule` is deliberately NOT registered here: test_bee_cli.mjs:2558 asserts every registry entry had its example executed, so a verb whose builder does not exist yet turns the repo red. It lands in cz-5 with its builder. THREE CHANGE SITES BEYOND THE REGISTRY, all of which a naive implementation misses: (a) skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_cli.mjs — `executedNames` grows only via explicit runExample/assertExampleOk calls (:148), so each new entry needs its own call or the coverage check at :2558-2563 fails; (b) skills/bee-hive/templates/bee.mjs:4830, the `state` group's `Unknown command … Use: …` list, which the DA5 bijection check at test_bee_cli.mjs:276-303 parses in BOTH directions — the new verbs must appear there; (c) FLAG_ALONE_BOOLEANS (bee.mjs:5067) already contains `json`, and these verbs add no other bare boolean, so nothing is needed there — stated so you do not go looking. compact-check's JSON output includes an anchor_missing check carrying the same command D10's nudge names, which is how the nudge stays reachable without a hook (D3). Then the D24 regen chain: mirror both edited runtime files to .bee/bin/, render the plugin trees, self-onboard, `release_manifest.mjs --write`. Author scripts/test_compact_verbs.mjs on the runBee/record conventions at scripts/test_conformance.mjs:44,95-97,51-55, with a negative-control row proving compact-check mutated nothing.

truths: Both verbs appear in `node .bee/bin/bee.mjs --help --json` with parameters, description and runnable examples derived from the registry entry.
test_bee_cli.mjs is green: both new entries had their examples executed, and the DA5 bijection holds in both directions.
state compact-check exits non-zero only on a usage error, never on a detected mismatch.
release_manifest --check and ledger_parity --check are green at cap time.
artifacts: Two registry entries, two handlers, two dispatch-map lines, the Use-line update, new test_bee_cli rows, refreshed mirrors and manifest, scripts/test_compact_verbs.mjs
prohibitions: Never register state compact-capsule here — its builder does not exist until cz-5 and the coverage gate is unconditional.

verify: node scripts/test_compact_verbs.mjs && node scripts/test_conformance.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_cli.mjs && node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --check && node scripts/ledger_parity.mjs --check

trace.outcome: Registered exactly two verbs — state compact-log and state compact-check — as thin CLI wrappers over lib/compaction.mjs (cz-3), following state.handoff.write's entry shape. All three named change sites landed: test_bee_cli.mjs example-execution rows for both verbs plus a mismatch-still-exits-0 row and a bad-event usage-error row; the state group's Use-list at bee.mjs:4830 now names compact-log/compact-check; FLAG_ALONE_BOOLEANS needed no change (json already present). compact-check's JSON output carries a distinct anchor_missing check (D10's predicate) alongside compactCheck's own presence-only anchor check, both naming the exact ANCHOR_NUDGE_COMMAND. state compact-capsule was NOT registered (its builder ships in cz-5). D24 regen chain ran in order: render_plugin_skill_trees -> onboard --apply -> release_manifest --write; .bee/bin/bee.mjs and .bee/bin/lib/command-registry.mjs mirrors are byte-identical to their sources. New scripts/test_compact_verbs.mjs drives the real .bee/bin/bee.mjs binary as a subprocess (test_conformance.mjs's runBee/record convention) with 7 scenarios, including a negative-control row proving compact-check mutates nothing under .bee/ across two consecutive runs (tree-hash proof, excluding the unrelated dispatcher-wide manifest-drift cache dir).
trace.verification_evidence: {"tests_inspected":"skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_cli.mjs (full 248-row suite, including its DA5 bijection tests and the unconditional every-example-executed coverage check at :2558-2563); scripts/test_conformance.mjs (10 black-box conformance scenarios over the shipped .bee/bin/bee.mjs binary, unrelated to this cell but part of its recorded verify chain).","tests_added_or_changed":"skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_cli.mjs gained 3 new checks: 'state.compact-log + state.compact-check examples run through the real dispatcher' (executes both registry examples, asserts D5 counts and the anchor_missing check's exact ANCHOR_NUDGE_COMMAND), 'state.compact-check exits 0 even when it reports a mismatch (D13)', and 'state.compact-log refuses (usage error, non-zero exit) on an unknown --event value'. New file scripts/test_compact_verbs.mjs: 7 scenarios over the real .bee/bin/bee.mjs subprocess — happy-path log, bad-event refusal (nothing written), missing --session-id refusal on both verbs (validate-args.mjs required-field path, distinct stdout-vs-stderr routing from a handler-thrown error), a detected-mismatch-still-exits-0 proof, the exact anchor_missing command match, and a tree-hash negative control proving two consecutive compact-check runs leave .bee/ byte-identical (excluding the unrelated dispatcher-wide manifest-drift cache).","red_failure_evidence":"Before the fix: `bee state compact-log`/`bee state compact-check` did not exist — running either against .bee/bin/bee.mjs returned 'Unknown command \"compact-log\"/\"compact-check\". Use: set, gate, worker, scribing-run, start-feature, lanes, session, handoff, advisor-ref.' (exit 1). scripts/test_compact_verbs.mjs and the new test_bee_cli.mjs rows would all fail against that state (entryByName would throw 'registry is missing entry \"state.compact-log\"'). During authoring, the missing-session-id assertions initially checked stderr and failed (status=1, stderr='') because validate-args.mjs's generic required-field refusal routes to stdout, unlike a handler-thrown Error (emitError) which routes to stderr absent --json — fixed by asserting stdout for that path. The mutates-nothing negative control also initially failed (before-hash != afterFirst-hash) because bee.mjs's own dispatcher-wide manifest-drift cache (.bee/cache/manifest-hash.json, unrelated to compaction) is written best-effort on every invocation and its directory springs into existence on the first call — fixed by excluding that one dispatcher-wide cache dir from the idempotence hash, since D13's guarantee is about compactCheck's own behavior, not the dispatcher's unrelated bookkeeping.","verification_run":"node scripts/test_compact_verbs.mjs && node scripts/test_conformance.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_cli.mjs && node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --check && node scripts/ledger_parity.mjs --check — full chain green, exit 0. test_compact_verbs.mjs: 7/7 PASS (ALL PASS). test_conformance.mjs: 10/10 PASS (ALL PASS). test_bee_cli.mjs: 248 passed, 0 failed. release_manifest --check: 484 file(s) match stored manifest. ledger_parity --check: .bee/bin/** matches the .bee/onboarding.json managed-hash ledger."}
trace.verify_output: test_compact_verbs.mjs: 7/7 PASS (ALL PASS). test_conformance.mjs: 10/10 PASS (ALL PASS). test_bee_cli.mjs: 248 passed, 0 failed (includes DA5 bijection for state.compact-log/state.compact-check, coverage-of-every-example check, and 3 new dedicated compact-log/compact-check rows). release_manifest --check: 484 file(s) match stored manifest. ledger_parity --check: .bee/bin/** matches the .bee/onboarding.json managed-hash ledger. Full chain exit 0.
